read_only_scope.hpc_access: read-only ssh commands with BatchMode and ConnectTimeout

entry_points.name: MotionSequence
entry_points.signature: MotionSequence(data: np.ndarray, target: str, reference: Optional[np.ndarray] = None)
entry_points.return_shape: dataclass; validates data is 2D with at least five columns [x,y,vx,vy,heading]

entry_points.name: estimate_ipv_pair
entry_points.signature: estimate_ipv_pair(primary: MotionSequence, counterpart: MotionSequence, *, history_window: int = 10, min_observation: int = 4, return_diagnostics: bool = False, diagnostic_steps: Optional[Sequence[int]] = None, solver_preset: str = 'accurate', solver_options: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None, max_workers: Optional[int] = None, candidate_executor=None, candidate_ipv_values: Optional[Sequence[float]] = None) -> Union[Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray], Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, Dict[str, List[Dict[str, np.ndarray]]]]]
entry_points.return_shape: (ipv_values, ipv_errors), each (T,2); optional diagnostics dict when return_diagnostics=True; current pre-min_observation rows are ipv=0.0,error=1.0

entry_points.name: estimate_ipv_current
entry_points.signature: estimate_ipv_current(primary: MotionSequence, counterpart: MotionSequence, *, history_window: int = 10, return_diagnostics: bool = False, solver_preset: str = 'parallel_accurate', solver_options: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None, max_workers: Optional[int] = None, candidate_executor=None, candidate_ipv_values: Optional[Sequence[float]] = None) -> Union[Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray], Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, Dict[str, List[Dict[str, np.ndarray]]]]]
entry_points.return_shape: (ipv_pair, err_pair), each (2,); optional diagnostics dict when return_diagnostics=True

entry_points.name: RealtimeIPVEstimator.__init__
entry_points.signature: RealtimeIPVEstimator(*, history_window: int = 10, solver_preset: str = 'parallel_accurate', solver_options: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None, max_workers: Optional[int] = None, candidate_executor=None, candidate_ipv_values: Optional[Sequence[float]] = None)

entry_points.name: RealtimeIPVEstimator.estimate_current
entry_points.signature: estimate_current(self, primary: MotionSequence, counterpart: MotionSequence, *, return_diagnostics: bool = False)

entry_points.name: RealtimeIPVEstimator.estimate_sign_current
entry_points.signature: estimate_sign_current(self, primary: MotionSequence, counterpart: MotionSequence, *, threshold: float = 0.05) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]

entry_points.name: Agent.estimate_self_ipv
entry_points.signature: estimate_self_ipv(self, self_actual_track, inter_track, *, return_details=False, solver_options=None, candidate_executor=None, candidate_ipv_values=None)
entry_points.return_shape: Mutates self.ipv and self.ipv_error; returns details dict only when return_details=True

current.name: cal_group_cost
current.form: vectorized collision factor, nearness, and np.sum aggregation

legacy_sigma01.name: cal_individual_cost
legacy_sigma01.form: loop over track points, np.amin(np.linalg.norm(cv - point, axis=1))

legacy_sigma01.name: cal_group_cost
legacy_sigma01.form: Python loop over relative velocities, if/else collision factor, incremental sum

diff_scope: a0fee535 rewrote legacy flat agent.py cost helpers from loop to vectorized form; current packaged lines preserve that vectorized form.
Prior isolation found restoring old loop helpers reproduced legacy diagnostic tracks and weights exactly; candidate-task refactor was not the cause.

likelihood_and_aggregation.likelihood: src/sociality_estimation/core/agent.py:917-980; active trajectory-similarity Gaussian product kernel with sigma=0.1

candidate_generation.lattice_status: lattice_planning is not on estimate_ipv_pair path; it is a simulation controller path at src/sociality_estimation/planning/lattice_planner.py:28-83

existing_knobs.name: solver_options
existing_knobs.accepted_values: Any dict passed through to scipy.optimize.minimize options for SLSQP
existing_knobs.effect: Overrides preset options; not a vectorization parity knob

existing_knobs.name: candidate_ipv_values
existing_knobs.accepted_values: Any numeric sequence coercible to np.asarray(..., dtype=float)
existing_knobs.effect: Changes candidate grid; for_realtime_sign sets five-candidate grid

existing_knobs.name: parallel_accurate
existing_knobs.effect: Same SLSQP defaults and same grid; enables ProcessPoolExecutor if no executor is supplied

sigma01_generation_params.git_subject: Run full InterHub IPV with sigma 0.1
sigma01_generation_params.solver: SciPy SLSQP defaults; no solver_preset/options existed

callers.how_invoked: deep-copied agents call estimate_self_ipv(nds_trj_lt, nds_trj_gs) and reverse
estimate_ipv_pair(artifacts.motion_lt, artifacts.motion_gs, optional return_diagnostics and diagnostic_steps)
estimate_ipv_pair(seq_primary, seq_secondary, history_window=HISTORY_WINDOW, min_observation=MIN_OBSERVATION, return_diagnostics=enable_diagnostics, diagnostic_steps=diag_steps)

selector_recommendation.recommendation: add solver_mode default='exact'; keep solver_preset as deprecated alias
selector_recommendation.reason: The new modes control cost backend, candidate grid, solver settings, and parallelism, so they are broader than the current solver_preset semantics.

fast.parallel_candidates: optional/online persistent executor; avoid nested pools in batch unless explicit

realtime.parallel_candidates: prefer persistent executor for online use

backward_compat.existing_callers_default: No explicit mode or preset should resolve to exact.

old_solver_preset_mapping.parallel_accurate: fast with seven-grid and candidate parallelism
old_solver_preset_mapping.balanced: fast with seven-grid and solver_options {'maxiter':20,'ftol':1e-3}; deprecated compatibility path
old_solver_preset_mapping.realtime: realtime; to preserve old seven-grid realtime, pass explicit seven-grid candidate_ipv_values

pipeline_plan: Record solver_mode and deprecated solver_preset in metadata.
Expose candidate grid override only if needed later.

assertions: exact equals frozen legacy values to atol <= 1e-16, rtol=0
fast equals exact to the same tolerance before calling fast same-answer
realtime returns finite candidate-range values and errors in [0,1]
old solver_preset aliases resolve as documented

risks: Changing default to exact intentionally changes current local outputs back to sigma01-compatible values.
Current solver_preset name is too narrow for composite estimator modes.
Candidate parallelism can oversubscribe when process_interhub already uses outer process pools.
ProcessPool task payloads need pickle-safe backend identifiers.
Module-level globals dt/TRACK_LEN/sigma/sigma2/grid are hidden couplings.
estimate_ipv_pair docstring says pre-min_observation rows are NaN but code returns 0/1.
The current realtime preset is solver-only; desired realtime mode also changes grid.
Fast vectorized equivalence may fail at 1e-16 unless reduction order is controlled.
Simulation code calls Agent.estimate_self_ipv directly, so backend selector must reach Agent too.
